zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gerard II於大約1255年出生在英國爾金斯利時，他的父親William Gerard I11253歲，母親Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley22歲。他於1280年之前與de Over Bradelegh在英國爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1316年在英國爾塔頓哈爾享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Gerard II于大约1255年出生在英国爾金斯利时，他的父亲William Gerard I11253岁，母亲Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley22岁。他于1280年之前与de Over Bradelegh在英国爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1316年在英国爾塔頓哈爾享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gerard II was born about 1255, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, his father, William Gerard I, was 11253 and his mother, Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, was 22. He married de Over Bradelegh before 1280, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1316, in Tattenhall, Cheshire, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Gerard II est né environ 1255, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William Gerard I, avait 11253 ans et sa mère, Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé de Over Bradelegh avant 1280, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé environ 1316, à Tattenhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Gerard II um 1255 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Gerard I 11253 und seine Mutter Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley 22. Er hat vor 1280 in Cheshire, England de Over Bradelegh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb um 1316 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Tattenhall, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Gerard II è nato il circa 1255 nel Kingsley, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, William Gerard I, aveva 11253 anni e il suo madre, Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato de Over Bradelegh prima del 1280, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì circa 1316, nel Tattenhall, Cheshire, England, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Gerard IIが約1255年にイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーで生まれた時，父William Gerard Iは11253歳，母Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsleyは22歳であった。1280年以前にイギリスチェシュアイアでde Over Bradeleghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は約1316年にイギリスチェシュアイアタッテンハルで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Gerard II(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 체실르 킨그슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gerard I(은)는 11253세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1280년 이전 영국 체실르에서 de Over Bradelegh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1316년 영국 체실르 타텐홀에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Gerard II nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Gerard I, tinha 11253 anos e sua mãe, Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com de Over Bradelegh antes 1280, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1316, em Tattenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Gerard II родился около 1255 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Gerard I было 11253 года, а матери, которую звали Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени de Over Bradelegh перед 1280 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер около 1316 г. в местности таттенолл, чешир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Gerard II nació aproximadamente 1255, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Gerard I, tenía 11253 años y su mamá, Emma De Kingsley Heiress Of Kingsley, tenía 22 años. Se casó con de Over Bradelegh antes de 1280, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1316, en Tattenhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años.
